steps.title: Find the slope of line s
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation $6x + 2y = 0$ in slope-intercept form:
$$2y = -6x$$
$$y = -3x$$
The slope of line $s$ is $m_s = -3$.

steps.title: Apply the perpendicularity condition
steps.content:
If line $t$ is perpendicular to line $s$, its slope is the negative reciprocal of $m_s$:
$$m_t = -\frac{1}{-3} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Use the slope formula with the two points
steps.content:
Line $t$ passes through $(7, 0)$ and $(k, \frac{4}{3})$ with slope $\frac{1}{3}$. Apply the slope formula:
$$\frac{\frac{4}{3} - 0}{k - 7} = \frac{1}{3}$$
Cross multiply:
$$\frac{4}{3} \cdot 3 = k - 7$$
$$4 = k - 7$$
$$k = 11$$
